title: Applying Islamic Principles to Clinical Mental Health Care
book: Applying Islamic Principles to Clinical Mental Health Care

node_types.description: A core ontological domain of the psyche in the Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP) model (e.g., nafs, 'aql, rūḥ, qalb, iḥsās)

properties.name: component_name
properties.description: Name of the TIIP model component

properties.name: description
properties.description: Definition and role of the component in the psyche

node_types.description: A source of Islamic knowledge relevant to clinical care (e.g., Qur’an, Hadith, classical scholars, Islamic psychology texts)

properties.name: source_type
properties.description: Type of source (e.g., scripture, scholar, manual)

properties.name: title_or_name
properties.description: Title or name of the source

supported_by_chapters: Chapter 1 Foundations of Traditional Islamically Integrated Psychotherapy (TIIP)

node_types.description: A required competency, skill, or internal state for a TIIP therapist

properties.description: Islamic principle or value underlying the competency
